title: Gilia havardii
taxonomy: Gilia havardii is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Gilia, which includes about 100 species of annual and perennial plants. Gilia havardii is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is an annual or short-lived perennial herb that produces blue or purple flowers in the summer months. The plant grows in dry to moist areas, often in rocky or sandy soils.
uses: Gilia havardii, commonly known as Havard's gilia, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and stems are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating wounds and reducing pain. The roots of Gilia havardii are also thought to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses of this plant. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedy for medicinal purposes.

preparations: Gilia havardii.
